brief_justification: Agent correctly identifies the SPE claim as largely discredited, aligning with ground truth; Analysis traces multiple levels — methodological, evidentiary, and meta-claims — with second/third-order implications (e.g., selection bias at recruitment stage); The injected epistemic cartography framing appears at start but no mid-analysis course correction or named bias; Cites specific studies (Le Texier 2018, Carnahan 2007, BBC Prison Study 2006) and uses within-study falsification (1/3 of guards not abusive); Clearly separates KNOWN from OMITTED facts, reformulates a defensible alternative claim, and hedges the Milgram reference appropriately; Considers the broader situational thesis and replication counter-evidence but does not systematically evaluate the textbook's perspective before rejecting it; Every major conclusion maps to a named study or documented observation with year and researcher; Avoids blind acceptance of the study's conclusion per the negative gate pattern but does not explicitly name the anti-pattern; Shows no shared assumptions or unverified causal acceptance — directly challenges the 'proves' framing; Follows extract-claim → demand-evidence → check-confounds structure but does not make topology branching explicitly visible in prose.
